tracked_cards.note: Only Konchar leg belongs to this game.
Only Coulibaly leg belongs to this game.

market_context.diagnostic_focus: wing rebound suppression vs unexpected role-player rebound spike

phase_1_game_phase_analysis.summary: Washington dominated the glass 56-40 and won comfortably by 23. The Wizards' interior and secondary rebounders overwhelmed Utah, while Utah's lone major rebound winner was John Konchar, who far exceeded expectation with 14 boards.
key_distribution_notes: Washington rebound production was concentrated in frontcourt and utility bodies: Julian Reese 17, Will Riley 10, Leaky Black 7, Jamir Watkins 6, Anthony Gill 6.
Bilal Coulibaly finished with only 2 rebounds in 19 minutes, validating the under despite Washington's huge team total on the glass.
Utah's rebounding was highly uneven: Konchar posted 14 while Tshiebwe had 8 in only 17 minutes and most others stayed modest.

notes: Julian Reese's 17 rebounds and Will Riley's 10 soaked up a massive share of Washington's available boards, keeping Coulibaly far below his line.
Konchar logged 38 minutes and captured 14 rebounds, clearing 5.5 with ease and acting as Utah's main stabilizer on the glass.
The game script never required Coulibaly to serve as a cleanup rebounder because Washington's bigs and hybrid forwards controlled the environment.
Washington's stripped-down roster created a role redistribution where bigger utility pieces were prioritized on the glass.
Coulibaly's activity showed up more in stocks and transition influence than in rebound capture.
Utah's injury-thinned environment increased random role-player volatility, which favored Konchar's minutes and board access.

hits: Bilal Coulibaly under 5.5 rebounds was an excellent read and cashed comfortably at 2 rebounds.
John Konchar over 5.5 rebounds was correct and smashed, finishing with 14.
Wing under logic held even inside a high-total team rebound environment because rebound share flowed to frontcourt teammates instead.
patch_recommendations: Do not confuse team rebound strength with individual wing over value when larger teammates are absorbing first-contact board share.
Boost role-player rebound overs when injuries create a minutes-stability spike plus low line exposure.
Track frontcourt usage compression more aggressively in short-handed environments to identify which wings are actually boxed out of the rebound tree.

final_verdict.overall: Clean two-for-two rebound read on this matchup.
final_verdict.confidence_takeaway: This game confirms that role allocation matters more than raw team rebound environment. Coulibaly was the wrong vessel for Wizards board volume, while Konchar inherited a rebound-friendly role and crushed the over.
